message:
test(mux): add B23g-o behavioral contract tests for 9 additional Cx cancellation and timeout paths

Expand the explicit-Cx cross-layer integration test suite from B23d-f
to B23o, covering all remaining public DirectMuxClient operations with
both pre-cancelled Cx and read-timeout scenarios. Each test exercises
the public API from an external test crate against a mock mux server.

Cancellation tests (B23g-j):
- B23g (get_lines): Pre-cancelled Cx fails before reading GetLines
  response. Mock server receives the request but client bails on read.
- B23h (write_to_pane): Pre-cancelled Cx fails before writing the
  WriteToPane payload. Verifies no post-handshake frame reaches server.
- B23i (single_render): Pre-cancelled Cx fails before reading render
  response. Validates the SingleRender request is sent but the response
  read is cancelled.
- B23j (send_paste): Pre-cancelled Cx fails before writing the paste
  data. Uses handshake_seen synchronization barrier.

Timeout tests (B23k-o):
- B23k (render_batch): Server stalls after handshake, client's Budget
  deadline fires before any render batch data arrives. Validates both
  timeout and cancellation as valid outcomes.
- B23l (subscription_cancel_shutdown): Tests graceful subscription
  teardown when the Cx budget expires during a long-running subscription.
  Server accepts handshake then stalls; client times out cleanly.
- B23m (single_render_read_timeout): Server completes handshake,
  receives SingleRender request, but never responds. Client's read
  times out after budget deadline.
- B23n (get_lines_read_timeout): Same pattern for GetLines — server
  receives request but stalls response.
- B23o (write_to_pane_read_timeout): Server stalls after handshake,
  client's WriteToPane times out during the write phase.

Shared infrastructure additions:
- Helper functions for each operation pattern (pre-cancel vs timeout)
- Updated section header from B21-B23f to B21-B23o
- Additional imports: GetLinesResponse, SingleRenderResponse

All tests gated on feature = "asupersync-runtime".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
